title: State of the product job market in early 2026
rawText:
View this post on the web at https://www.lennysnewsletter.com/p/state-of-the-product-job-market-in-ee9

👋  Hey there, I’m Lenny. Each week, I answer reader questions about building product, driving growth, and accelerating your career. For more: Lenny’s Podcast [ https://substack.com/redirect/ccfc62d3-5bc3-4dcc-ac1f-796ea2aeab32?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ] | Lennybot [ https://substack.com/redirect/1e48527a-cd9d-478d-859a-e5fd5947cedf?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ] | How I AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/6ab07e16-557b-48e8-ba07-a6e8f06a73de?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ] |  My favorite AI/PM courses [ https://substack.com/redirect/d8067491-601d-4339-b70f-5723a0693a0a?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ], public speaking course [ https://substack.com/redirect/17667f25-a91b-4a66-8ab4-271b6d098032?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ], and interview prep copilot [ https://substack.com/redirect/208c6c74-241a-4004-8520-68a2cc163250?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ]
P.S. Get a full free year of Lovable, Manus, Replit, Gamma, n8n, Canva, ElevenLabs, Amp, Factory, Devin, Bolt, Wispr Flow, Linear, PostHog, Framer, Railway, Granola, Warp, Perplexity, Magic Patterns, Mobbin, ChatPRD, and Stripe Atlas by becoming an Insider subscriber [ https://substack.com/redirect/7065f952-a71d-4676-8ddd-30230ba4244c?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ]. Yes, this is for real [ https://substack.com/redirect/bc609f42-fd5a-4723-bcb6-1a0650bf1377?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ].
Welcome to our biannual State of the Product Job Market—our fourth and, very surprisingly, the most optimistic. In spite of the headlines about layoffs and AI taking jobs, we’re actually seeing a lot of promising signs in tech hiring, and some interesting new trends:
PM openings are at the highest levels we’ve seen in over three years
AI hasn’t slowed the demand for software engineers (at least not yet)
AI roles in general are absolutely exploding
Design roles have plateaued
The Bay Area is increasing in importance
Remote work opportunities continue to decline
Despite ongoing layoffs, the overall number of tech jobs continues to grow
While these numbers are promising, I know a lot of people are having a hard time finding a job right now. And more openings doesn’t automatically mean people are finding jobs more quickly. For anyone in that situation, first of all, I’m sorry. Second, I’m working on ways to help. Until then, check out the end of this post for a bunch of resources I’ve collected that’ll improve your odds of landing a gig.
Let’s get into it.
This analysis is based on data from TrueUp [ https://substack.com/redirect/bb357974-8f9f-48fd-a2a2-c72e98c94d9f?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ], one of my favorite collaborators and sources of data. They track job openings at tech companies and top startups around the world (over 9,000 companies) and make it easy to browse open gigs [ https://substack.com/redirect/8c2fa574-4654-4fcd-96fa-75173b36fb75?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ]. Their data looks at roles at tech companies—the most sought-after and lucrative jobs. (It doesn’t include roles at non-tech companies and consulting agencies.)
1. PM openings are at the highest levels we’ve seen in over 3 years
There are over 7,300 open PM roles at tech companies globally, and trending up. This is 75% above the low we saw in early 2023, and already up nearly 20% since the start of this year. Today we have the most open PM roles we’ve seen since 2022. (You can see all of these open roles here [ https://substack.com/redirect/24e88cc8-933e-4c36-aa7b-3f494ee7cd16?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ].)
The same trend is true for engineering roles . . .
2. AI hasn’t slowed the demand for software engineers (at least not yet)
There are over 67,000 (!!!) eng openings [ https://substack.com/redirect/c1993646-a859-4183-a752-dd58476914b8?j=eyJ1IjoiNW9wenJ4In0.nThRJwzSHl8--qnjuKIb6701J257PyFcUoQ-1b5aIEg ] at tech companies globally right now, with 26,000 just in the U.S. We don’t know if there would have been more open roles if not for AI or if AI is actually leading to more open roles, but since the start of this year, the increase in open eng roles is accelerating even more.
If you’re skeptical that this growth is real and likely to be sustained, we’re also seeing a surge in demand for tech recruiters. The number of open recruiter roles is almost back to 2022 peak levels. This role got hit the hardest post-Covid, and also recovered the quickest. By definition, recruiting headcount expands and contracts with hiring demand, so it’s likely a leading indication that we’re tracking toward
